verbatim_comment: Love the selection of organic snacks. Hard to find this variety anywhere else. The almond butter collection alone is worth the membership.
Delivery took 6 days this time. I can get the same stuff on Amazon Fresh tomorrow. Not worth paying a membership fee for slow shipping.
Thrive is the only place I trust for clean supplements. I've tried everything and keep coming back. The quality control is real.
App is painfully slow on my Android phone. Takes 5+ seconds to load search results. I end up just ordering on desktop instead.
Great prices on pantry staples. Wish you had more fresh produce options though — I still have to go to the grocery store for fruits and vegetables.
The Keto filter is a lifesaver. I just wish the app remembered my dietary preferences so I don't have to set the filter every single time.
My last two orders had items missing. Support was helpful but it's frustrating to not get what you paid for.
Best membership I have. Better than Amazon, better than Costco for organic. The curation is what makes it — you don't carry junk.
Good product selection but the website search is frustrating. I search for 'GLP-1 friendly' and get random results that aren't actually GLP-1 compliant.
Cancelled my membership because managing the subscription is way too confusing. Tried to skip a month and accidentally ordered. The subscription management page is a maze.
Love the new snack brands you've been adding. The discovery experience is fun — I always find something new.
Delivery is fine, prices are fine, but nothing about the experience feels personalized. It's the same homepage for everyone.
Switched from Misfits Market to Thrive and never looked back. The quality is noticeably better, especially for supplements.
The app crashes when I try to apply multiple dietary filters at once. I'm Keto AND dairy-free, and the app can't handle it.
Your GLP-1 selection has been growing and I love it. I'm on Ozempic and Thrive is the only grocery app that takes this diet seriously.
Prices have gone up on a lot of my regular items. Starting to feel less like a deal and more like Whole Foods online.
Three delivery complaints in a row. Last package arrived with a crushed box and a leaking bottle of olive oil. Done.
The product reviews are super helpful. I trust the community here more than Amazon reviews.
Wish you had a meal planning feature. I know what diets I follow but I don't know what to cook with the products I buy.
Why can't I manage my subscription from the app? Every time I need to change my auto-ship I have to go to desktop. It's 2026.
Started using Thrive specifically for the Paleo selection. Nothing comes close. Even my local Whole Foods doesn't carry half of this.
App is so slow on mobile that I've given up browsing. I only order from my saved list now. You're losing sales from me because the app can't keep up.
The fact that you vet every product you sell is the reason I'm a member. I don't want to read ingredient labels — I trust you to do it.
Good store but I keep seeing the same products. Where's the novelty? I want to be surprised sometimes.
Love that you carry niche brands. Found my favorite adaptogen blend here that I can't get anywhere else.
Subscription management is a nightmare. Tried to cancel and it took 4 clicks through confusing modals. Then I got charged again the next month. Furious.
The dietary filter for Whole30 is a gamechanger. Doing my third round and Thrive makes the shopping so much easier.
Delivery times are inconsistent. Sometimes 3 days, sometimes 7. I can't plan around that.
Mobile app search is broken. I type 'protein bars' and the first result is a face cream. What?
Just recommended Thrive to 5 friends. The membership pays for itself in 2 orders if you buy the right stuff.
Solid selection but I wish the email recommendations were actually personalized. I'm vegan and you keep sending me beef jerky promotions.
Checkout freezes on mobile at least once a week. I have to close the app and start over. Lost items from my cart multiple times.
The price comparison feature is brilliant. Seeing Thrive vs. Whole Foods pricing right on the product page builds trust.
Thrive's private label snacks are surprisingly good. Didn't expect house brand to compete with the premium stuff.
Delivery complaint #4. Package left in the rain, everything soaked. I'm paying for a premium membership and getting a budget experience.
Fine for basics but your fresh/refrigerated options are terrible compared to what's available now. Amazon Fresh same-day has better fresh selection.
GLP-1 member here — I tell everyone on my Ozempic support group about Thrive. You guys are the best for high-protein, clean ingredient snacks.
The Android app is borderline unusable. Scrolling is janky, images load slowly, and the filter panel is laggy. iOS gets all the love.
Been a member for 3 years. The product quality has only gotten better. Keep doing what you're doing.
Love Thrive but I always have to supplement with a local grocery run for fresh stuff. Would be amazing if you expanded into fresh produce delivery even in limited markets.
I can manage everything about my life from my phone except my Thrive subscription. The subscription portal feels like it was built in 2015.
Your customer support is genuinely great. Had an issue with a damaged item and they replaced it plus sent a surprise gift. That kind of touch keeps me loyal.
Prices are creeping up. When I joined 2 years ago the savings vs. Whole Foods were clear. Now it's closer to even on many items.
I'm a nutritionist and I recommend Thrive to all my clients. The dietary filtering makes it easy to send them specific product links.
Tried to browse on my phone yesterday — search took 8 seconds to return results. Closed the app and ordered on Amazon instead. You're losing me.
The Gluten-Free selection is the best I've found online. As a celiac, I feel safe shopping here.
Wish there was a way to see what other people with my same diet are buying. Like a 'popular with Keto members' section.
Just used the referral program — my friend got a great deal and I got credits. Win-win. The product basically sells itself.
The email I get every week has zero relevance to what I actually buy. It's the same generic 'top picks' that don't match my diet at all.
Solid 7 out of 10. Good selection, fair prices, room for improvement on mobile speed and personalization. I'll stick around.
